summary: To calculate the child mortality rate, it includes all ages from birth up to but not including five years old based on widely recognized medical standards that emphasize this critical period during which many life-threatening illnesses occur most frequently among infants and preschool-aged kids. Agent1 determined through a thorough analysis of available data that the Child Mortality Rate typically considers children between zero and four years of age as per established public health guidelines emphasizing heightened risk factors within these early developmental stages leading into pre-school years where significant morbidity often manifests itself clinically or statistically impacting overall survival statistics significantly more than older childhood populations do at those later ages beyond infancy proper yet still under school entry criteria generally accepted internationally today regarding such vital measurements tracking global progress toward reducing preventable deaths amongst youth globally speaking broadly across different regions worldwide facing
